title: Runtime Sanitizers, Dynamic Analysis, and Language Profiles · Policy Clinic: Sanitizer Ci
summary: Add runtime instrumentation to native and managed-language test profiles. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates sanitizer ci increment in a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Run expensive dynamic checks in a documented matrix with symbolized actionable output.
Use runtime warnings, leak checks, fuzzing, and framework diagnostics for managed languages.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Sanitizer Ci evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-sanitizer-ci-19mk77: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sanitizer Ci into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-sanitizer-ci-19mk77:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
